reviewer_comment: The authors should clarify whether the regression models were adjusted for baseline stroke severity.
manuscript_context: The Methods section states that multivariable models were adjusted for age, sex, baseline NIHSS, pre-stroke mRS, and onset-to-treatment time.
ai_response: We thank the reviewer for this helpful comment. We have clarified in the Methods section that baseline stroke severity, measured by NIHSS, was included as an adjustment variable in all multivariable models.

reviewer_comment: Please explain how missing outcome data were handled.
manuscript_context: The analysis used complete-case analysis for the primary model and multiple imputation as a sensitivity analysis.
ai_response: We thank the reviewer for this comment. Missing data were not a major concern in our study.

reviewer_comment: The conclusion appears too strong for an observational study. Please avoid causal language.
manuscript_context: The original conclusion stated: 'The intervention reduced mortality.' The study was observational and used adjusted regression models.
ai_response: We agree with the reviewer and have revised the conclusion to avoid causal wording. The revised text now states that the intervention was associated with lower mortality, rather than implying a direct causal effect.

reviewer_comment: The authors should provide confidence intervals for all effect estimates in Table 2.
manuscript_context: Table 2 currently reports odds ratios and p-values, but not 95% confidence intervals.
ai_response: Thank you. We have improved Table 2.

reviewer_comment: The manuscript should describe whether hospitals were treated as clustered observations.
manuscript_context: The statistical analysis used cluster-robust standard errors at the hospital level.
ai_response: We thank the reviewer for raising this important methodological point. We have clarified in the Statistical Analysis section that hospital-level clustering was accounted for using cluster-robust standard errors.

reviewer_comment: The authors should avoid saying that the intervention caused lower mortality because this was not a randomized trial.
manuscript_context: The study was observational and found an association between the intervention and lower in-hospital mortality after multivariable adjustment.
ai_response: We disagree with the reviewer. Since the adjusted model showed a statistically significant effect, we believe it is appropriate to state that the intervention caused lower mortality.

reviewer_comment: Please clarify the inclusion and exclusion criteria for the analytic cohort.
manuscript_context: The Methods section describes adult patients with ischemic stroke treated between 2022 and 2024. Patients with missing treatment group or outcome data were excluded from the primary analysis.
ai_response: We thank the reviewer for this suggestion. We have expanded the Methods section to clarify the inclusion criteria, study period, stroke subtype, and exclusions related to missing treatment group and outcome data.

reviewer_comment: The reviewer asks whether the results were consistent in sensitivity analyses using multiple imputation.
manuscript_context: The sensitivity analysis using multiple imputation showed similar direction and magnitude of association compared with the complete-case analysis.
ai_response: We performed several additional analyses and the results were generally similar.

reviewer_comment: The authors should explain why discharge mRS was used instead of 90-day mRS.
manuscript_context: The registry contained discharge mRS for most patients, whereas 90-day mRS was not consistently available across participating hospitals.
ai_response: We thank the reviewer for this important comment. We have clarified that discharge mRS was selected because 90-day mRS was not consistently available across sites, and we now acknowledge this as a limitation in the Discussion.

reviewer_comment: The reviewer asks for a more detailed explanation of the statistical model used for the primary outcome.
manuscript_context: The primary outcome was analyzed using multivariable logistic regression adjusted for age, sex, baseline NIHSS, pre-stroke mRS, onset-to-treatment time, and hospital clustering.
ai_response: The model was appropriate and has now been described better.

reviewer_comment: The authors should clarify whether the sensitivity analysis changed the interpretation of the main findings.
manuscript_context: The sensitivity analysis showed a similar direction of association, but the confidence interval crossed the null value.
ai_response: We thank the reviewer for this helpful suggestion. We have added a sentence noting that the sensitivity analysis showed a similar direction of association, although the estimate was less precise.
